intro: Neocussonia lukwangulensis, also known as Lukwangula tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Neocussonia lukwangulensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tonics, which are often used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
